A two-halled house with only the western and southern halls
is known as SiddhSrtha; one with the western and northern halls
Tamasurya; one with the northern and eastern halls, Danda; one with
the eastern and southern halls, Vdta; one with the eastern and west-
ern ones, Grhd'culll (or Culll); and one with the southern and
northern ones, Kdca. The first type of house leads to acquisition of
wealth; the second, to the owner's death; the third, to death by
being beaten with clubs (or, to punishment and death); the fourth,
to constant grief from strife; the fifth, to loss of money; and the
sixth, to enmity with kinsmen.
